chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يُجْزِئُ مِنْ حَجَّةِ الْإِسْلَامِ وَ مَا لَا يُجْزِئُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 38 – What suffices from the Hajj of Al-Islam and what does not suffice

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/162)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/162)
text: رَجُلٍ أَعْتَقَ عَشِيَّةَ عَرَفَةَ عَبْداً لَهُ أَ يُجْزِئُ عَنِ الْعَبْدِ حَجَّةُ الْإِسْلَامِ قَالَ نَعَمْ قُلْتُ فَأُمُّ وَلَدٍ أَحَجَّهَا مَوْلَاهَا أَ يُجْزِئُ عَنْهَا قَالَ لَا قُلْتُ أَ لَهُ أَجْرٌ فِي حَجَّتِهَا قَالَ نَعَمْ
قَالَ وَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ ابْنِ عَشْرِ سِنِينَ يَحُجُّ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ حَجَّةُ الْإِسْلَامِ إِذَا احْتَلَمَ وَ كَذَلِكَ الْجَارِيَةُ عَلَيْهَا الْحَجُّ إِذَا طَمِثَتْ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Shihab,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who freed a slave of his on the eve of Arafat. Would a Hajj be sufficed from the slave, a Hajj of Al-Islam?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes’. I said, ‘So if a mother of a child is taken to Hajj by her master, would it suffice from her?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No’. I said, ‘Is there any Recompense for him regarding her Hajj?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘And I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about a boy of ten years performing Hajj’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Upon him is the Hajj of Al-Islam when he attains puberty, and similar to that is the girl, upon her would be the Hajj when she (starts) menstruating’.
en.sarwar: 8. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from ibn Mahbub from Shihab who has said the following: “A man set free his slave in the evening of the day of ‘Arafah (ninth of Dhul Hajjah); if the slave’s al- Hajj is sufficient for Hajjahta al-Islam. He (the Imam) said, ‘No, it is not sufficient.’ I then asked, ‘Will he receive any reward for it?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘Yes, he receives rewards.’ I asked about a ten-years old boy and his al-Hajj. He (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, said, ‘He owes Hajjahta al-Islam when he grows to adulthood and so also is the case of a young girl.’”
